subject: ObjectBroker runtime pricing
observation: PC runtime license price per seat

subject: ObjectBroker Unix development pricing
observation: Unix development toolset price per seat

subject: ObjectBroker NT development pricing
observation: Windows NT development seat price

subject: ObjectBroker runtime pricing model
observation: Runtime priced on per-machine basis structured by hardware/OS combination with volume discounts

subject: ObjectBroker version at publication
observation: Current generally available version at time of study publication

subject: Platform count
observation: ObjectBroker supports 19 hardware/operating system combinations

subject: CORBA compliance version
observation: ObjectBroker 2.5 conforms to CORBA specification version 1.2

subject: CORBA 2.0 roadmap
observation: Digital working on CORBA 2.0 compliance for ObjectBroker; CORBA 2.0 defines inter-ORB communication

subject: IDL language bindings in v2.5
observation: ObjectBroker 2.5 IDL language bindings
value_text: C binding, Visual Basic DLL binding

subject: IDL C++ binding roadmap
observation: ObjectBroker 2.6 will provide a CORBA 2.0 IDL-to-C++ binding

subject: ObjectBroker first release year
observation: ObjectBroker first introduced in 1991 providing long history of production use

subject: NAS inclusion
observation: ObjectBroker runtime included as part of Digital NAS installed on over half of all Digital systems sold

subject: Core component: ORB Core
observation: ObjectBroker core components include ORB Core for routing object requests

subject: Core component: Repositories
observation: ObjectBroker includes Repositories for storing IDL / IML / MML definitions

subject: Core component: Registries
observation: ObjectBroker includes Registries containing configuration information

subject: Core component: Context Objects
observation: ObjectBroker includes Context Objects providing information about each user and system

subject: Graphical utilities
observation: ObjectBroker 2.5 graphical utilities included in product
value_text: Systems Administrator, Network Tester, Context Object Editor, Repository Manager, Implementation Viewer, OLE Network Portal, DDE Listener

subject: Skeleton Server
observation: ObjectBroker Skeleton Server wraps existing callable APIs with CORBA-compliant interface
value_text: Wraps callable APIs with IDL interface

subject: Script Server
observation: ObjectBroker Script Server encapsulates command line interfaces with CORBA interface

subject: OLE Network Portal
observation: OLE Network Portal in ObjectBroker 2.5 for Windows locates OLE-aware servers in ObjectBroker registry and presents OLE 2.0 interface

subject: DDE Gateway
observation: ObjectBroker DDE Gateway accepts DDE messages from legacy DDE-aware applications

subject: Legacy system encapsulation
observation: ObjectBroker wraps legacy systems with CORBA-compliant OO interface; encapsulated procedural code appears as independent objects

subject: Application Partitioning via IML/MML
observation: Digital IDL extensions IML and MML allow identification of object implementations and selection among multiple implementations
value_text: IML: describe object implementations; MML: select among implementations

subject: CORBA/OLE coexistence strategy
observation: Aberdeen assesses OLE-COM will dominate Windows desktops and NT servers while CORBA will focus on Unix servers; ObjectBroker bridges both

subject: ObjectBroker 2.6 announcement
observation: ObjectBroker 2.6 formally announced August 1995 with general availability expected early 1996

subject: v2.6 feature: Secure ORB
observation: ObjectBroker 2.6 will use GSS API for DCE to enable Kerberos-level security

subject: v2.6 feature: OLE Automation
observation: ObjectBroker 2.6 OLE Automation will allow OLE applications to access data and functionality of remote CORBA objects
value_text: Remote CORBA object access from OLE clients (e.g. Excel)

subject: v2.6 feature: C++ binding
observation: ObjectBroker 2.6 will provide IDL-to-C++ binding for native C++ development

subject: v2.6 feature: Systems Management APIs
observation: ObjectBroker 2.6 adds APIs for non-Digital systems management tools and customer applications

subject: v2.6 feature: Quick Start
observation: Quick Start capability automatically generates basic application framework by reading IDL; running client-server skeleton in 10 minutes

subject: v2.6 feature: Better Performance
observation: ObjectBroker 2.6 includes significant performance improvements alongside new features

subject: v2.6 new platform: IBM MVS
observation: ObjectBroker 2.6 will support IBM MVS mainframe; first ORB to directly support MVS

subject: v2.6 new platform: OS/400
observation: ObjectBroker 2.6 forthcoming support for IBM OS/400

subject: v2.6 new platform: Windows 95
observation: ObjectBroker 2.6 forthcoming support for Windows 95

subject: v2.6 new platform: Silicon Graphics IRIX
observation: ObjectBroker 2.6 forthcoming support for SGI IRIX (port done by Logica)

subject: Unique multi-platform breadth claim
observation: Aberdeen states Digital is the only major systems supplier that can claim 19-platform ORB coverage
value_text: No competitor matches 19-platform breadth per Aberdeen

subject: CORBA market supporter count
observation: CORBA specification supported by over 450 companies including all major hardware and software suppliers

subject: OLE-CORBA integration lead
observation: Aberdeen characterizes Digital as a driving force in linking OLE and CORBA standards; can provide OLE-CORBA integration today (1995)

subject: Microsoft partnership
observation: Digital has established strong strategic partnership with Microsoft on OLE-CORBA integration standards work with OMG

subject: Multi-billion dollar services advantage
observation: Aberdeen notes Digital is a multi-billion dollar full-services firm providing a services/support advantage over pure software ORB vendors

subject: Primary customer segment
observation: ObjectBroker customers primarily are user organizations needing systems integration; companies unable to easily extend existing applications

subject: Initial industry verticals
observation: Digital initial installations concentrated in banking/financial services and discrete/process manufacturing sectors
value_text: Banking, financial services, discrete manufacturing, process manufacturing

subject: ISV target segment
observation: ObjectBroker attracting independent software vendors who want to integrate complementary 3rd-party applications for competitive differentiation

subject: Target audience scope
observation: Aberdeen states ObjectBroker is useful to IS organizations AND independent software vendors AND systems integrators

subject: Fortune 1000 heterogeneous infrastructure
observation: Most Fortune 1000 companies have multiple deployed systems from proprietary mainframes to Unix servers to desktop PCs requiring integration
value_text: Heterogeneous multi-vendor enterprise IT is the norm

subject: Systems integration as primary developer work
observation: Systems integration described as a major assignment for developers in building enterprise infrastructure
value_text: Integration is a dominant development activity

subject: ORB expected criticality growth
observation: Aberdeen predicts ORBs will become increasingly critical as more companies look to integrate disparate systems

subject: Demand growth forecast
observation: Digital expects demand to increase quickly over the next 12 to 24 months and is deploying resources accordingly

subject: Aberdeen overall verdict
observation: Aberdeen concludes ObjectBroker is highly strategic for Digital and has support of senior management; one of few areas with consistent investment during cost cutting

subject: Tandem port attribution
observation: Tandem Integrity and NonStop Kernel ports of ObjectBroker are the result of work by Logica North America Inc; all other implementations done by Digital
